narrative: Markets were range-bound into the Jan. 30 close as investors weighed mixed mega-cap tech earnings and a steady Fed, then de-risked late week on news around President Trump’s Fed chair pick (Kevin Warsh), which boosted the dollar and hit precious metals hard. The S&P 500 finished slightly up on the week, while small caps lagged as Russell 2000 momentum cooled and cyclicals/commodities reversed into month-end.

value_picks.name: PayPal Holdings, Inc.
value_picks.why_undervalued: Trading near its 52-week low around the week ending 2026-01-30, PYPL was priced for little growth despite a large, profitable payments franchise.
value_picks.bull_case: If branded checkout stabilizes and cost cuts persist, multiple expansion plus steady buybacks could drive a multi-year re-rate.

value_picks.name: Old National Bancorp
value_picks.why_undervalued: Regional bank sentiment remained pressured, and ONB screened as materially below estimated fair value in late Jan 2026 despite solid profitability.
value_picks.bull_case: Normalization in rate expectations and credit costs could support a return toward peer multiples and improved earnings power.

value_picks.name: Huntington Bancshares
value_picks.why_undervalued: Screened as significantly below estimated value in late Jan 2026 amid rate and regulatory overhangs affecting large regionals.
value_picks.bull_case: If NIM stabilizes and expenses stay controlled, earnings resilience plus dividend can support a recovery trade.

value_picks.name: TowneBank
value_picks.why_undervalued: Appeared on a Jan 2026 undervaluation screen, reflecting depressed regional bank valuations vs modeled fair value.
value_picks.bull_case: Loan growth and benign credit could help close the discount as investors reprice community banks.

value_picks.name: Seven Hills Realty Trust
value_picks.why_undervalued: Screened as deeply discounted to estimated fair value in Jan 2026 as CRE fears weighed on the sector.
value_picks.bull_case: Stabilizing credit markets and improved funding conditions could narrow the valuation gap and support dividends.

momentum_picks.name: Corning Incorporated
momentum_picks.catalyst: Shares rallied sharply into late January 2026 on expectations of rising AI-driven optical/connectivity demand and improving outlook.
momentum_picks.risk_reward: Strong trend but sensitive to guidance/AI capex cycle shifts.

momentum_picks.name: Teradyne, Inc.
momentum_picks.catalyst: Semiconductor test spending momentum and bullish positioning into 2026 supported outsized relative performance.
momentum_picks.risk_reward: High beta to semicap cycle; pullbacks likely if orders soften.

momentum_picks.name: Western Digital Corporation
momentum_picks.catalyst: Storage upcycle expectations (AI data centers) drove continued upside into late Jan 2026.
momentum_picks.risk_reward: Cycle turns can be abrupt; watch NAND pricing and inventory.

momentum_picks.name: Comfort Systems USA, Inc.
momentum_picks.catalyst: Continued strength in commercial/industrial HVAC and data-center related buildout kept momentum strong into late Jan 2026.
momentum_picks.risk_reward: Valuation risk if construction spending slows.

momentum_picks.name: Seagate Technology Holdings
momentum_picks.catalyst: HDD demand tied to hyperscale/AI storage and improving pricing expectations supported strong performance.
momentum_picks.risk_reward: Competitive/technology shifts can compress margins quickly.

watchlist_updates.detail: Tractor Supply reported Q4 EPS $0.43 missing estimate $0.46, revenue $3.9B lagging by 2.74%; shares dropped 7.6%.
Mastercard Q4 adjusted EPS $4.76 beat $4.2, revenue $8.8B beat by 0.80%; shares rose 4.3%.
Caterpillar Q4 adjusted EPS $5.16 beat $4.67, revenue $19.13B beat by 6.60%; shares rose 3.4%.
Newmont dropped over 11.5% as gold prices fell nearly 10% on profit-taking after Trump's Fed chair nomination.
Microsoft shares plunged 10% on disappointing Q4 results amid software sector pressure.
